rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. DANNER III et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-537224 WITH CASE CGC-10-501981
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 2, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-537224 WITH CASE CGC-10-501981. Denied without prejudice. If a new trial is eventually granted in the Danner matter, Defendants may re-file their motion for consolidation. As to the discovery issue raised in reply, Defendants may file a new motion seeking this relief. Any such motion should be noticed in the discovery department. The Court encourages the parties to meet and confer about using the Danner discovery in the Barsotti case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY HOLTZ VS. GROUPON, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 2, 2014, Line 5, CROSS COMPLAINANT GROUPON, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication. The motion is denied in its entirety. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant is immune from liability pursuant to 47 USC sec. 230. Specifically, there is a dispute of fact regarding whether defendant created the information at issue (or materially contributed to its creation) or whether "the information at issue [was] provided by another information content provider.'" Delfino v. Agilent Technologies, Inc. (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 790, 804-805. There is a triable issue of material fact as to the sixth cause of action for violation of CC 3344. A dispute of fact exists whether defendant should be treated like the owner/mere webhost or the advertiser/creator of the information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VIN DEBUT LLC VS. SQUAT AND GOBBLE CAFE, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 2, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANTS SQUAT AND GOBBLE CAFE, LLC, ISSA SWEIDAN'S Joinder In Motion To Consolidate. The motion is granted in part. The court orders the 5 subrogation actions (cases CGC-13-535195, CGC-13-535876, CGC-13-536381, CGC-537218, and CGC-14-539565) to be onsolidated for trial purposes. The parties are also ordered to coordinate all discovery and motion practice. Case CGC-13-535195 is designated the lead case number. The motion is denied as to the Vin Debut case (case CGC-13-530607). Vin Debut has a close trial date and consolidation will lead to a prejudicial delay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
